pages.raw_text:
Apparently but little local inter-
est is being taken in the election
by merchants and business men
generally. Some Of the corpora
tons secured nominations satisfac-
tory to themselves, but have taken
but little active part in the cam
paign, it is stated, beyond the usual
contributions.


The union labor vote of the state
would appear to be big factor in
the present fight and If the work
Ing men stand with the American
Federation Of Labor there is rea-
son to believe that the big republi-
can majorities of the past will be
cut down to a greater extent than
for years past.


The slums Of all the cities in the
state are well registered and will
as a rule support the republican
ticket in order to claim protection
and any other favors which are so
necessary to this element from the
dominant party.


The farmers grange has made an
active campaign and has worked
for the first time in the history Of
the state hand in hand with the
State Federation of Labor for the
initiative and referendum and the
direct primary laws. If the present
